news_list.title: PM on for high-stakes fuel, gas meeting
news_list.summary:
Anthony Albanese is setting off on a two-day trip to Singapore in efforts to shore up Australia's fuel stockpile from one of the nation's largest suppliers, as his government courts key Asian trading partners to mitigate the economic shocks of the war in the Middle East.

Singapore is Asia's largest oil trading hub and the world's sixth-largest refinery export hub.

Its exports make up 26 per cent of Australia's total refined fuel stockpile. Of this, Singapore provides 55 per cent of the nation's petrol, 22 per cent of its jet fuel and 15 per cent of its diesel.

In return, Australia is Singapore's second-largest supplier of liquefied natural gas (LNG) and provides the Indo-Pacific country with 32 per cent of its supply.

While Qatar is Singapore's biggest gas supplier, prices have soared after a Qatari gas facility was extensively damaged by Iranian missiles in late March. It has subsequently increased global demand for Australian LNG.

Friday's meeting between the Prime Minister and his Singaporean counterpart, Lawrence Wong, will build on the existing deal signed in March, where both countries committed to maintaining trade flows and to consult each other over any disruptions impacting energy trade.

Part of that deal also included addressing import and export restrictions and it is understood these settings, in the context of a constricted market, will be on the upcoming meeting's agenda.

The joint talks will follow Mr Albanese's visit to Jurong Island, a 3000-hectare offshore island near Singapore's CBD that is home to the country's three main refineries, alongside more than 100 global companies involved in the oil or petrochemical trade.

The island's enormous refining infrastructure allows it to process more than 800,000 barrels per day. It means that, despite having no domestic crude oil reserves, Singapore's refining output has drawn keen trade interest from its Asian neighbours and Europe.

Prior to the war, Singapore imported about 70 per cent of its crude oil from the Middle East but in recent years has diversified its supply mix to include Africa, Malaysia, India and Vietnam.

On Tuesday, Mr Albanese said the trip - which had been brought forward in the year - would be an "important progression". He said trading relationships with Asian partners paid "dividends at difficult times".

" ... Because we've been able to have those conversations and work together, which is what we will do," he said.

The Singapore trip comes days after Mr Albanese spoke with Chinese Premier Li Qiang to discuss the "importance of energy security".

A readout of the conversation provided by the Prime Minister's Office said the pair had "agreed to increase government-to-government communication so that Australia and China are both working in support of regional energy security".

China, which supplies about a third of Australia's jet fuel, has restricted refined fuel exports following the outbreak of war.

It follows international reports that Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi is planning a trip to Australia to discuss the Strait of Hormuz, along with co-operation on security and rare-earth minerals.

Last week, Australia had gained assurances from Japan, South Korea, and Singapore that Asian exporters would continue to provide fuel supplies to Australia as per usual, despite the conditions in the Middle East.

Energy Minister Chris Bowen said this week Australia's fuel supply was secure "well into" May.

It comes as the federal government implemented a number of emergency measures over the last few weeks, including the adoption of a National Fuel Security Plan, temporary slashing of the fuel excise, expanded powers for the commonwealth to underwrite additional fuel cargoes, and relaxing of fuel standards.

Originally published as Anthony Albanese to meet with Singapore PM Lawrence Wong

news_list.title: No fuel relief in sight despite ceasefire
news_list.summary:
Mixed messages on the Strait of Hormuz being open or closed leaves Australia hoping a "fragile" ceasefire holds, Foreign Minister Penny Wong says.

The key shipping route is central negotiations between the US and Iran, who tentatively agreed to a two-week ceasefire on Wednesday Australian time with negotiations brokered primarily by Pakistan expected later in the week.

The Strait's closure has driven up fuel prices across the globe; news of the ceasefire saw oil prices plunge more than 15 per cent overnight.

Even if negotiations between the US and Iran cement the ceasefire, Senator Wong says disruptions to fuel supply will be long-lasting because of the six weeks the Strait has been closed.

"Not only is the ceasefire fragile and it has to hold, even if we see a successful negotiation, we've still got a lot of disruption in the system," she said.

"We've got the gap in supply. And then we have, from the Strait being closed, then we have the damage to infrastructure in the region, which is obviously affecting supply.

"So we've got a long way to go."

Ms Wong spoke to her counterparts in Australia's major fuel and LNG trading partners on Wednesday night - Singapore, Korea, Malaysia and Japan.

"We continue to work with them on how we can mutually assure each other's supplies," she told the ABC earlier on Thursday morning.

"Our partners want to supply us, but we all know what they need is more supply coming through the Middle East and from elsewhere."

The ceasefire deal announced Wednesday does not include Lebanon. Israel bombarded Lebanon overnight; Lebanon's civil defence ministry said at least 254 people were killed, while the Israel Defense Forces say it was targeting Iran-backed Hezbollah targets.

Multiple Iranian state news outlets then reported the Strait had been closed in response, in the early hours of Thursday Australian time.

The White House then insisted the Strait was in fact open. "We have seen an uptick in traffic in the Strait," White House press secretary Karoline Leavitt said. "It's something we are monitoring minute by minute, hour by hour."

The New York Times reports Beijing pushed Tehran to accept a ceasefire, as China buys much of Iran's oil and is being dragged down by a deep-rooted property crisis, curbs in economic growth and steep youth unemployment.

Asian commodities analyst, Stephen Innes, said markets were treating the ceasefire as a pause, not as peace.

"Beneath the surface, the physical oil market continues to tell a very different story. The damage from the supply shock has already been done," he said.

"Inventories have been drawn down, tanker flows remain disrupted, and cost frictions such as insurance premiums and logistical bottlenecks have not disappeared with the headline."

The way Iran and the US are talking about the Strait points to "controlled movement rather than full normalisation", he said in a note.

"Safe passage tied to coordination and technical limitations reads less like reopening and more like rationing. Enough flow to prevent panic but not enough to surrender leverage."

Beijing's want for a ceasefire was clear, Mr Innes said.

"It is here that China quietly enters the frame as the invisible hand behind the pause.

"This ceasefire did not emerge in a vacuum. It was forced through the system by the economic pressure of disrupted energy flows, which hit Asia hardest and, by extension, China's growth engine at the worst possible time.

"Tehran did not come to the table out of goodwill, it came because the cost of not doing so risked losing its most important economic backstop. That dynamic matters because it means the ceasefire is not just geopolitical, it is also economic triage."

news_list.title: Malaysia's fuel prices to stay high despite ship release
news_list.summary:
PETALING JAYA: Malaysians may have breathed a sigh of relief after one of seven Malaysian-owned commercial vessels stranded in the Strait of Hormuz was granted safe passage earlier this week. But experts caution that domestic petrol and oil prices are unlikely to drop immediately.

Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has assured that Malaysia has sufficient petroleum supply to withstand global energy shocks in the short term, with no immediate risk of disruption at least until June.

However, Centre for Market Education CEO and economist Carmelo Ferlito warned that supply stability does not automatically translate into lower consumer prices.

"The prime minister's statement essentially refers to physical availability, not necessarily price stability. Malaysia is unlikely to face shortages or rationing in the short term.

"But having adequate supply does not mean domestic fuel prices will fall, because prices are shaped not only by availability but by global market conditions and expectations," he said.

Ferlito explained that oil and petrol prices remain elevated despite the safe passage of Malaysian vessels because the global oil market is forward-looking and sensitive to risk.

"Prices reflect expectations about future disruptions, not only current flows. The Strait of Hormuz remains a critical chokepoint and even temporary tensions could push prices higher.

"This uncertainty is priced into oil through risk premiums, regardless of whether specific shipments eventually pass safely," he said.

He also highlighted the role of maritime costs.

"Geopolitical tensions increase insurance premiums, freight rates and security costs, all embedded into the final price of oil and refined products. These costs are significant and persist as long as uncertainty remains in the region."

Ferlito stressed that Malaysia operates within an integrated global energy market.

"Even with stable domestic supply, prices are influenced by international benchmarks. Local developments - such as the release of Malaysian ships - have very limited impact on global pricing, and consequently on domestic prices."

The ripple effect extends beyond fuel.

"Energy costs feed into production chains. Higher oil prices, whether due to real scarcity or perceived risk, affect transportation, food, and other goods. Adjustments are gradual but unavoidable," he added.

From a geopolitical angle, International Islamic University Malaysia international relations expert Assoc Prof Dr Mohd Yazid Zul Kepli said Malaysia's diplomacy was key in securing the vessels' release.

"Malaysia's role demonstrates effective protection of national shipping, strengthens its image as a constructive regional actor and shows the practical value of maintaining functional relations with all parties, including Iran, during a crisis," he said.

Mohd Yazid added that Malaysia's neutral but friendly stance with Iran allowed it to negotiate "de-escalatory, case-specific solutions" for its vessels, unlike the confrontational approaches taken elsewhere.

"By securing safe passage through diplomacy rather than confrontation, Malaysia appears capable of protecting its shipping interests through measured statecraft in one of the world's most sensitive maritime chokepoints."

He added that the incident could enhance Malaysia's reputation in global maritime security and open doors for future trade or energy discussions with Iran.

"The longer-term impact will depend on whether Malaysia can maintain its neutral posture while retaining confidence among Gulf partners, major energy suppliers and other maritime stakeholders."

Iran's statement that it "does not forget its friends" reinforces Malaysia's consistent approach of acting fairly and neutrally, boosting its credibility in regional diplomacy.

Ferlito said while some easing in prices may occur if tensions subside, "as long as uncertainty in the region persists, it is reasonable to expect that prices will remain relatively high in the short term, even without physical shortages".

news_list.title: Close watch on tourism backbones
news_list.summary:
PUTRAJAYA: A comprehensive evaluation of the aviation and hospitality sectors will be carried out to ensure the success of the Visit Malaysia 2026 campaign, which has now been formally extended through to the end of 2027, says Datuk Fahmi Fadzil.

The Communications Minister said the Cabinet was briefed on the extension, originally proposed by Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi.

Fahmi highlighted that the Tourism, Arts and Culture Minister has identified a pressing need to safeguard the country's tourism pillars from the dual pressures of the global energy crisis and ongoing geopolitical tensions in the Middle East.

"The Cabinet was informed today of the announcement made by Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi regarding the extension of Visit Malaysia 2026 until the end of 2027.

"The Tourism Minister has also advised the Cabinet that there is a necessity to scrutinise both the aviation and hospitality sectors," he said during a press conference here yesterday.

He said the government's intervention aims to prevent these critical industries from becoming overburdened by rising operational costs and shifting travel patterns.

"This scrutiny is vital to ensure these sectors are not too heavily impacted by the global energy crisis and the conflict in West Asia," he added.

themes.name: 地緣政治停火影響與美元匯率動態
themes.insight: 美伊兩國的停火協議引發市場對地緣政治風險的擔憂緩解，並直接導致美元走勢重挫。同時，中東局勢的持續不確定性也對日圓等避險貨幣造成區間震盪的影響，凸顯了地緣政治事件對主要貨幣的敏感性。
evidence: 1.美、伊停火兩周，引發美元重挫
4.中東局勢未明，日圓區間震盪
5.美國就業報告前，美元維持堅挺

themes.name: 第二季市場結構性輪動與投資策略
themes.insight: 市場分析師預期第二季將迎來「3重力量」，為捕捉更大輪動機會提供契機。此外，在經歷評價修正後，全球股市被認為存在潛在的投資價值，這表明市場正關注第二季的板塊輪動機會與結構性變化。
evidence: 2.第二季「3重力量」　捕捉更大...
3.柏瑞投信：歷經評價修正後，全...

themes.name: 澳洲能源安全與東南亞貿易夥伴關係
themes.insight: 澳洲總理前往新加坡，尋求鞏固國家的燃料儲備，並加強與亞洲貿易夥伴的聯繫，以緩解中東衝突帶來的經濟衝擊。此次訪問凸顯了在當前全球能源危機下，區域內能源供應鏈的穩定性和貿易夥伴關係的重要性。
evidence: PM on for high-stakes fuel, gas meeting
No fuel relief in sight despite ceasefire

themes.name: 馬來西亞燃料價格與全球能源市場連動
themes.insight: 儘管部分馬來西亞擁有的商船獲得霍爾木茲海峽安全通行，但專家預警國內油價短期內不太可能立即下降。這顯示全球能源市場的價格不僅受當前供應影響，更受未來預期和地緣政治風險溢價的影響，馬來西亞作為全球能源市場的一部分，其國內價格仍受國際基準影響。
evidence: Malaysia's fuel prices to stay high despite ship release

themes.name: 馬來西亞觀光策略與全球能源危機應對
themes.insight: 馬來西亞政府宣布將「旅遊馬來西亞 2026」活動延長至 2027 年底，同時將對航空和酒店業進行全面評估。此舉旨在應對全球能源危機和中東地緣政治緊張局勢對旅遊業造成的潛在壓力，確保關鍵產業的穩健發展。
evidence: Close watch on tourism backbones

items.summary: 美伊宣布 2 週停火後，全球金融市場迅速做出反應，股市強勢反彈，道瓊週三飆漲超 1300 點，國際油價暴跌超 10%，高盛資深交易員 Rich Privorotsky 指出，目前並非追價標普 500 指數的理想時機。

items.title: 美伊停火已現破裂跡象 中東局勢再添變數
items.summary: 《TheStreet》報導，在美國總統川普周二與伊朗達成為期兩周的停火協議後，全球一度鬆了一口氣。各國領袖紛紛表達肯定，投資人也開始重新布局，拋售原油、轉而買進股票。

"The longer-term impact will depend on whether Malaysia can maintain its neutral posture while retaining confidence among Gulf partners, major energy suppliers and other maritime stakeholders."

Iran's statement that it "does not forget its friends" reinforces Malaysia's consistent approach of acting fairly and neutrally, boosting its credibility in regional diplomacy.

Ferlito said while some easing in prices may occur if tensions subside, "as long as uncertainty in the region persists, it is reasonable to expect that prices will remain relatively high in the short term, even without physical shortages".

items.title: Close watch on tourism backbones
items.summary:
PUTRAJAYA: A comprehensive evaluation of the aviation and hospitality sectors will be carried out to ensure the success of the Visit Malaysia 2026 campaign, which has now been formally extended through to the end of 2027, says Datuk Fahmi Fadzil.

The Communications Minister said the Cabinet was briefed on the extension, originally proposed by Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi.

Fahmi highlighted that the Tourism, Arts and Culture Minister has identified a pressing need to safeguard the country's tourism pillars from the dual pressures of the global energy crisis and ongoing geopolitical tensions in the Middle East.

"The Cabinet was informed today of the announcement made by Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi regarding the extension of Visit Malaysia 2026 until the end of 2027.

"The Tourism Minister has also advised the Cabinet that there is a necessity to scrutinise both the aviation and hospitality sectors," he said during a press conference here yesterday.

He said the government's intervention aims to prevent these critical industries from becoming overburdened by rising operational costs and shifting travel patterns.

"This scrutiny is vital to ensure these sectors are not too heavily impacted by the global energy crisis and the conflict in West Asia," he added.

topics.topic: 馬來西亞燃料價格與全球能源市場連動
topics.summary: 儘管部分馬來西亞擁有的商船獲得霍爾木茲海峽安全通行，但專家預警國內油價短期內不太可能立即下降。這顯示全球能源市場的價格不僅受當前供應影響，更受未來預期和地緣政治風險溢價的影響，馬來西亞作為全球能源市場的一部分，其國內價格仍受國際基準影響。
evidence: Malaysia's fuel prices to stay high despite ship release

topics.topic: 馬來西亞觀光策略與全球能源危機應對
topics.summary: 馬來西亞政府宣布將「旅遊馬來西亞 2026」活動延長至 2027 年底，同時將對航空和酒店業進行全面評估。此舉旨在應對全球能源危機和中東地緣政治緊張局勢對旅遊業造成的潛在壓力，確保關鍵產業的穩健發展。
evidence: Close watch on tourism backbones

ai_analysis.full_text:
2026-04-09 (週四) 盤前晨報
【今日結論】
今日台股預計將因美股強勁反彈及台積電 ADR 大漲而高開，主要受美伊停火協議緩解地緣政治緊張氣氛的影響，推動風險資產回歸。然而，市場仍需密切關注美國經濟衰退的跡象與地緣政治風險的潛在反覆，這些因素可能在盤中帶來獲利了結的壓力。
【新聞驅動】
美伊同意停火兩週成為昨夜市場最大催化劑，顯著降低了地緣政治風險對油價及全球供應鏈的衝擊，直接帶動了美股的普遍上漲。英特爾因多重利多股價飆升，成為半導體族群強勢的關鍵指標，而 Meta 推出的新 AI 模型亦顯示該領域競爭依舊激烈。儘管 CNN 恐懼貪婪指數仍處於「恐懼」區域，但這與美股的強勁走勢形成對比，可能預示著市場情緒存在滯後或部分資金仍在觀望。
【國際與台股】
昨夜美股三大指數全面走高，S&P 500 上漲 2.51%，Nasdaq 漲 2.80%，費半更是飆升 6.34%。其中，台積電 ADR 大漲 5.96%，理論台股價格大幅溢價，加上台指期夜盤與富台指期均上漲，顯示今日台股開盤動能強勁。東北亞市場方面，日經 225 小幅上漲 0.03%，KOSPI 則有 0.82% 的漲幅，整體呈現穩健承接的態勢，與美股的樂觀情緒相互呼應。
【KOL與情緒】
CNN 恐懼貪婪指數顯示市場情緒偏向「恐懼」(30)，然而加密貨幣的恐懼貪婪指數僅為 17，顯見市場風險偏好正在逐步回歸。社群熱搜關鍵字如「力積電」和「00988a」顯示散戶對特定標的的關注度正在提升，而「外匯存底」、「外汇储备」等話題則可能與全球經濟前景或貨幣政策預期有關，這些熱點的出現預示著散戶情緒的轉向或新的投機動能正在集結。
【操作含意】
今日操作應聚焦於「風險資產反彈」及「半導體業」的結構性走強。停火協議降低了地緣政治不確定性，使投資者得以更關注經濟基本面。儘管美國非農就業數據疲弱暗示衰退風險，但市場對停火的正面反應表明短期利多暫時壓制了微觀環境的壓力。操作上可留意半導體相關個股，並適時關注美股期貨及市場情緒指標，以應對盤中可能出現的波動。

chart_orchestration.overall_summary: 今日台股預計高開，受美股強勁反彈及台積電 ADR 大漲帶動，主因美伊停火協議緩解地緣政治緊張。市場關注風險資產回歸，但需留意美國經濟衰退跡象與地緣政治風險的潛在反覆可能帶來的獲利了結壓力。新聞主題顯示，地緣政治停火影響了美元走勢，並有利於科技股，特別是半導體族群。社群與科技主題熱度也圍繞著類似的焦點。然而，整體市場情緒仍有分歧，部分指標顯示恐懼。

chart_gap_suggestions.why_needed: 把衝突事件數、致死數與未來風險預測可視化，讓地緣政治對市場的影響更直觀。
chart_gap_suggestions.evidence: 專案已有 ACLED service，但目前未做成可視化圖表。

chart_gap_suggestions.why_needed: 把 PTT 與 Dcard 的熱門討論、看多看空比與主題熱度做成圖，補足市場情緒面。
chart_gap_suggestions.evidence: 現有 trend_pulse 與社群抓取已能提供底層資料。

chart_gap_suggestions.why_needed: 晨報常聚焦 AI / 半導體 / 科技權值，將科技主題與新聞來源拆開呈現會更清楚。
chart_gap_suggestions.evidence: 目前科技內容散落在新聞主題與 AI 分析中，尚未獨立成圖。
